title: Diallobus tora
taxonomy: Diallobus tora is classified as a genus of flowering plants in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. The class is Magnoliopsida, and the order is Gentianales. Diallobus tora is further classified as a member of the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. Further research may be needed to determine the exact classification within this family.
benefits: Diallobus tora is known to have various health benefits. It is believed to possess antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing inflammation and fighting free radicals in the body. The plant is also said to have anti-diabetic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it potentially useful in managing conditions such as diabetes and arthritis. Additionally, Diallobus tora has been reported to have antimicrobial properties, which can aid in preventing infections and promoting wound healing. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and confirm its efficacy.
